body.text: On February 20, 2026, I stood in front of a judge and became Sage J Phoenix — officially, legally, permanently.
But the truth is, the rebirth was well into the making.
In mid-2024, it started with a name. I tried Sage on for size — which I'd done before, looking for something that fit.
But this one was different. It didn't feel borrowed. It didn't cause pain, like my given name did.
It felt like coming home to the person I never had permission to be.
On 11/11/2025 — Veterans Day — I walked into the courthouse and filed the paperwork to make it official. Believe it or not, I didn't plan that date — the Universe did.
Two months later, on January 9, 2026 — exactly on the day I joined the Air Force 23 years prior — I signed a contract with a new business partner.
A new mission was launched. A new chapter was starting. I felt it to my core.
My brain started tingling. I could feel my insides burning, radiating — full of energy.
Exactly one week later, it was my (re)birthday — I turned 48. Every moment. Every day. Every week just kept confirming the rebirth.
Then on 02/20/2026, I stood in front of the judge and was granted my name change.
I walked out of that courthouse as Sage J Phoenix — with every document, credit card, and piece of paper that followed a little mini-celebration.
Every piece of mail with my new name was the Universe whispering to me: This is you. It was always you. You deserve this. It's your time.
This is you. It was always you. You deserve this. It's your time.
It's only been in this time that I've realized the acceptance I've been searching for — the identity I tried to find with others — was the wrong place to look.
There's only one place it could come from: the inside. And I finally gave myself permission to be totally, completely, unapologetically me.
I am Sage J Phoenix. I have walked through the fire, burned my old self to the ground, and come out reborn.
None of the old baggage. None of the old weight. Only the good. Only the positive.
The mission is clear. I am laser focused. It is time to leave a legacy.
